hypotheses.label: Unnamed | Unnamed | Unnamed | Unnamed | Unnamed
hypotheses.hypothesis: The innermost k-core (k=8) contains 10 nodes (2.0% of graph). Types: {'information': 10}. This resembles a policy monopoly (Sabatier & Jenkins-Smith, 1993) — an entrenched coalition controlling resources and framing. The robustness gap (-0.1834) indicates the system degrades faster under targeted removal than random failure, confirming dependency on this core.
hypotheses.action: Don't challenge the core directly. Fund venue-shopping: peripheral advocacy groups that can reframe the dominant issue image and shift debates to alternative forums. Identify secondary-belief alignments where peripheral coalitions can form tactical alliances.

hypotheses.label: 286 peripheral nodes, 1 high-confidence GNN links
hypotheses.hypothesis: The network has 286 peripheral nodes (degree 1-2) — spare capacity for rewiring. There are 1 high-confidence GNN-predicted links and 3 that would merge components. Adding these links could integrate stranded nodes without triggering systemic resistance.
hypotheses.action: Prioritize the 3 component-merging links to reduce fragmentation. Design joint initiatives that pair peripheral nodes with core actors.

hypotheses.label: Surf Club ↔ Mental Health & Wellbeing
hypotheses.hypothesis: Linking 'Surf Club' to 'Mental Health & Wellbeing' (probability=0.626) could reinforce collaboration. This is a 'Project alignment' recommendation.
hypotheses.action: Design a joint initiative requiring partnership between these two entities.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Linking 'Focus Ireland' to 'Liquid Therapy' (probability=0.737) could reinforce collaboration. This is a 'Actor coordination' recommendation.
